session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback data and classifying it according to the guidelines.

{
  "success": true,
  "pr_number": 8869,
  "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8869",
  "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from 9 erk-learn plans",
  "review_threads": [
    {
      "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yu0Wp",
      "path": "docs/learned/architecture/health-check-runner-gateway.md",
      "line": 55,
      "is_outdated": false,
      "comments": [
        {
          "author": "github-actions",
          "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — private method name in prose.

Source: `src/erk/core/health_checks/managed_artifacts.py:72`

Risk: `_load_artifact_allowlist()` is a private (`_underscore`) implementation detail that can be renamed without notice. Per source-pointers.md tripwires, private methods must never appear in documentation prose. The behavior it implements (merging allowlist from both config files into a frozenset) is what matters, not the method name.

Suggested fix: Replace the private method name reference with a source pointer:
```
<!-- Source: src/erk/core/health_checks/managed_artifacts.py, _load_artifact_allowlist -->
See `managed_artifacts.py` for allowlist loading — reads `[artifacts].allow_modified` from both config files and merges into a frozenset.
```",
          "created_at": "2026-03-07T04:34:35Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-06 20:34:41 PT

Found 1 violation across 1 file. Inline comment posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Verbatim code blocks — None found (CLI usage and commit format blocks are acceptable exceptions)
✅ Inaccurate claims — Existing thread (PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5ytCxs) on health-check-runner-gateway.md:27 already present; not re-posted
❌ Drift risk — Found in docs/learned/architecture/health-check-runner-gateway.md:55 (private method `_load_artifact_allowlist()` named in prose)

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/architecture/health-check-runner-gateway.md:55`: Private method `_load_artifact_allowlist()` named in prose — should be replaced with source pointer

### Files Reviewed
- `docs/learned/architecture/health-check-runner-gateway.md`: 1 violation (private method drift risk; existing inaccuracy thread already present on line 27)
- `docs/learned/architecture/progress-feedback-threading.md`: 0 violations (prior issues resolved)
- `docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-prepare.md`: 0 violations (prior issues resolved)
- `docs/learned/cli/commands/reconcile.md`: 0 violations (prior issues resolved)
- `docs/learned/planning/incremental-dispatch.md`: 0 violations
- `docs/learned/testing/alias-verification-pattern.md`: 0 violations (policy update is HIGH VALUE)
- `docs/learned/testing/test-file-organization.md`: 0 violations (prior drift risk resolved, new table clean)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-06 20:34:41 PT**: Audited 7 docs: 1 drift risk (private method `_load_artifact_allowlist()` in health-check-runner-gateway.md:55). All other docs clean or previously resolved issues.
- **2026-03-06 16:47:47 PT**: Audited 7 docs: 1 drift risk (test counts table in test-file-organization.md). Previously flagged issues already addressed or have existing threads.

</details>",

## Summary
The pr-feedback-classifier skill has analyzed PR #8869 and identified:
- **1 actionable thread** (review feedback): A drift risk in `docs/learned/architecture/health-check-runner-gateway.md` at line 55 where a private method name (`_load_artifact_allowlist()`) is referenced in prose. Per documentation standards, this should be replaced with a source pointer instead.
- **4 informational discussion comments**: These are system-generated messages from the GitHub Actions workflow (plan queuing, workflow status, implementation start, and audit results), which are informational in nature and do not require action.
- **Complexity**: The single actionable item is classified as `local` (single-line change), which can be auto-proceeded.
- **File restructuring**: The PR only adds new documentation files, with no renames or moves detected in the git diff.
